behavior: Soars with wings held in a shallow V, often rocking side to side; rides thermals with few wingbeats and searches for carrion chiefly by smell.
field_marks: Large, dark brown-black vulture with a small, bare head.
Adults show a red, wrinkled head and a pale hooked bill.
Long, broad wings have conspicuously fingered tips.
In flight, the wings are held in a shallow V (dihedral).
Underwings are two-toned: dark leading edge and paler silvery trailing flight feathers.
Long tail extends beyond the toe tips in flight.
habitat: Open and semi-open country, including mixed farmland, rangeland, forest edges, and other landscapes with carrion; roosts in secluded trees, cliffs, rocks, and similar elevated sites.
